verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A (Heat Shock Protein 70, Hsp70) is a canonical molecular chaperone functioning in the cytoplasm and nucleus. It is a soluble ATPase that binds hydrophobic peptide sequences on misfolded proteins. No documented membrane anchor, no GPI attachment, no stable integration into PM or endomembrane systems. Under stress or cell death, Hsp70 can be released into the extracellular space via lysis or unconventional secretion, but this is a transient, non-anchored export; no wash-resistant surface retention. No evidence of lysosomal exocytosis-mediated surface display. Not cell-state-dependent on the PM. Not reported as a target of surface-directed ADC, antibody, or CAR-T programs seeking membrane-proximal engagement (extracellular Hsp70 is detected in serum and extracellular vesicles, but as freely diffusible cargo, not surface-immobilized). Cytoplasmic/nucleoplasmic resident protein with no surface accessibility pathway.
